text.title: Founder Led Qualification Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Founder Led Qualification Rubric. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Qualification MUST produce 5 binary (yes/no) checks: budget, authority, need, timeline, fit. No 'maybe' state.
rule.rationale: Binary checks eliminate the 'kind of' ambiguity that overqualifies prospects.

rule.statement: Each yes MUST cite evidence (quote, named contact, numeric value). Yes without evidence defaults to no.
rule.rationale: Without evidence, the rubric is a wish-list.

rule.statement: 3+ no's MUST auto-disqualify the prospect; no proposal effort proceeds.
rule.rationale: Even 2 no's is often a no-go; the threshold prevents sunk-cost extension.

rule.statement: Any check the runner cannot decide in 60s defaults to no until evidence is gathered.
rule.rationale: Default-yes is overqualification; default-no forces clarity.

rule.statement: Rubric is applied to all proposal-ready prospects in one weekly batch (not per-incoming-lead).
rule.rationale: Batch application enforces consistency and prevents emotional drift across the week.
